POL-477.title: IMPLEMENT: Polaris Self-Optimization Loop
POL-477.body:
## Objective

Implement the Self-Optimization Loop (SOL) roadmap produced from [POL-462](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-462/analyze-rename-auto-research-loop-to-self-optimization-loop), evolving the current dev-gated Auto Research/autoresearch scoring feature into a broader evidence-driven optimization subsystem.

## Context

[POL-462](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-462/analyze-rename-auto-research-loop-to-self-optimization-loop) analyzed how Polaris should rename and expand Auto Research Loop into SOL. The existing `src/autoresearch` module scores completed runs and routes human-reviewed proposals, while related Worker Router and QC analyses reserve future SOL inputs through [POL-469](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-469/emit-router-telemetry-and-sol-scoring-inputs) and [POL-476](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-476/feed-qc-metrics-into-sol-scoring-and-operator-reports).

This IMPLEMENT parent is itself a child of [POL-462](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-462/analyze-rename-auto-research-loop-to-self-optimization-loop) and should preserve the analyze-only boundary of the source issue. SOL must observe, score, report, and recommend. It must not silently mutate runtime behavior or bypass deterministic Polaris orchestration.

Related issues: [POL-460](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-460/analyze-polaris-worker-router-informed-by-9router), [POL-461](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-461/analyze-polaris-quality-control-architecture), [POL-463](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-463/implement-polaris-worker-router), [POL-470](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-470/implement-polaris-quality-control-lifecycle).

## Goal

Deliver a staged SOL implementation that renames the operator-facing concept, preserves Auto Research as a sub-capability, expands evidence and scoring models, persists historical performance, and generates review-gated routing/runtime recommendations.

## Scope

* smartdocs/raw/analysis/**
* smartdocs/specs/raw/**
* smartdocs/specs/active/**
* src/autoresearch/**
* src/cli/**
* src/types/**
* src/loop/**
* src/cluster-state/**
* src/finalize/**
* src/config/**
* tests covering SOL/autoresearch, CLI, loop evidence, config, and reporting behavior

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] SOL architecture is documented with boundaries against uncontrolled self-mutation.
- [ ] Auto Research naming is migrated or aliased under SOL while preserving backward compatibility.
- [ ] SOL consumes Foreman, worker, router, QC, validation, token, runtime, and intervention evidence from durable Polaris artifacts.
- [ ] Foreman and worker scoring models expose overall and diagnostic sub-scores grouped by task type, repo, route, role, risk, provider, and model where evidence exists.
- [ ] Historical performance storage and reports support routing recommendations without replacing Foreman validation.
- [ ] Polaris-repo self-improvement behavior creates review-gated analyze/implementation proposals only when explicitly allowed.
- [ ] All child issue validation passes.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/autoresearch src/cli src/loop src/cluster-state src/config

## Ordering

Children execute in dependency order: architecture and naming first, evidence/schema next, scoring and history after schema, then recommendation routing and Polaris-specific self-improvement behavior.

## Non-goals

* Do not silently mutate runtime policy, provider config, skills, doctrine, or source code from SOL output.
* Do not replace Worker Router, QC, Foreman validation, Closeout Librarian, or finalize gates.
* Do not make SOL depend on one QC provider or on 9Router.
* Do not remove the existing `polaris autoresearch` command until compatibility aliases and migration docs exist.

POL-476.title: Feed QC metrics into SOL scoring and operator reports

POL-469.title: Emit router telemetry and SOL scoring inputs

POL-460.title: ANALYZE: Polaris Worker Router informed by 9Router

POL-483.title: Generate SOL routing recommendations and self-improvement proposals
POL-483.body:
## Objective

Generate SOL routing recommendations and Polaris-specific self-improvement proposals from historical scores and evidence.

## Context

[POL-462](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-462/analyze-rename-auto-research-loop-to-self-optimization-loop) requires SOL to recommend worker routing, provider/model choice, role assignment, policy updates, trust/cost thresholds, and Polaris runtime improvements. In the Polaris repository, SOL may create analyze or implementation issues; in other repos it must stay advisory and repo-agnostic.

## Goal

Add recommendation generation and proposal routing that remains review-gated, deterministic, and policy-controlled.

## Scope

* src/autoresearch/**
* src/cli/autoresearch.ts
* src/cli/index.test.ts
* src/config/**
* src/tracker/** only if issue-routing integration is needed
* smartdocs/specs/raw/**
* tests covering recommendation generation, policy gates, dry-run output, and Polaris-dev-only proposal routing

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] SOL generates explainable routing recommendations from score trends and grouping dimensions.
- [ ] Recommendations include evidence references, confidence, affected route/task/provider/model, and proposed policy action.
- [ ] Consumer repos receive advisory output only by default.
- [ ] Polaris-dev context may file review-gated analyze or implementation proposals when explicitly configured or invoked.
- [ ] Dry-run mode shows all proposed tracker mutations without writing to Linear.
- [ ] Tests prove SOL does not silently mutate runtime policy, config, docs, or source files.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/autoresearch src/cli src/config

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-482](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-482/add-sol-historical-performance-store-and-reports) being Done first.
* Consumes router/QC scoring inputs from [POL-469](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-469/emit-router-telemetry-and-sol-scoring-inputs) and [POL-476](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-476/feed-qc-metrics-into-sol-scoring-and-operator-reports) when available.

## Non-goals

* Do not silently change provider policy, worker routing, or trust thresholds.
* Do not create implementation issues outside the Polaris development repository.
* Do not bypass human review for SOL-generated work.
* Do not depend on one QC provider or on 9Router.

POL-482.title: Add SOL historical performance store and reports
POL-482.body:
## Objective

Add SOL historical performance storage and operator reports for run, route, worker, provider, model, task type, and repository trends.

## Context

SOL recommendations need history, not just a single-run diagnosis. The store should derive from durable Polaris artifacts and stay local/reviewable by default.

## Goal

Persist normalized SOL score snapshots and expose reports that summarize trends and grouping dimensions without introducing remote analytics.

## Scope

* src/autoresearch/**
* src/cli/autoresearch.ts
* src/cli/index.test.ts
* src/config/**
* .polaris/clusters/** documentation references only as needed
* smartdocs/specs/raw/**
* tests covering storage, report generation, config defaults, and backward compatibility

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] SOL can persist score snapshots under a deterministic local artifact path.
- [ ] Reports group scores by repo, route, task type, role, risk, provider, model, worker id, and time window when evidence exists.
- [ ] Report output supports JSON and human-readable CLI modes.
- [ ] Storage is append-only or generation-safe and does not rewrite historical run evidence.
- [ ] Config defaults preserve current behavior and do not enable remote analytics.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/autoresearch src/cli src/config

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-481](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-481/implement-sol-foreman-and-worker-scoring-models) being Done first.

## Non-goals

* Do not upload SOL telemetry to external services.
* Do not replace run ledgers or cluster state as source-of-truth evidence.
* Do not auto-apply routing changes from historical reports.

POL-481.title: Implement SOL Foreman and worker scoring models
POL-481.body:
## Objective

Implement SOL scoring models for Foremen and workers with visible diagnostic sub-scores.

## Context

[POL-462](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-462/analyze-rename-auto-research-loop-to-self-optimization-loop) asks for Foreman scoring across token usage, runtime duration, intervention frequency, dependency handling, dispatch accuracy, evidence validation, scope control, completion, and failure recovery. It also asks for worker scoring across token usage, runtime duration, validation, QC findings, repair iterations, scope adherence, acceptance criteria, and first-pass success.

## Goal

Extend the existing binary gate scoring foundation into SOL score reports that preserve detailed sub-scores and confidence rather than flattening everything into a single opaque number.

## Scope

* src/autoresearch/**
* src/types/**
* src/cli/autoresearch.ts
* src/cli/index.test.ts
* smartdocs/specs/raw/**
* tests covering scoring formulas, missing evidence, confidence, and grouping behavior

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Foreman score includes token, duration, intervention, pre-analysis, dependency, dispatch, evidence validation, scope, completion, and recovery dimensions where evidence exists.
- [ ] Worker score includes token, duration, validation, QC, repair iteration, scope adherence, acceptance criteria, and first-pass dimensions where evidence exists.
- [ ] Scores include confidence and skipped/unavailable reasons for missing evidence.
- [ ] Existing binary gate diagnosis remains available for compatibility.
- [ ] Tests cover high-confidence scoring, partial evidence scoring, and no-evidence fallback behavior.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/autoresearch src/cli

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-480](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-480/define-sol-evidence-schema-and-aggregation-inputs) being Done first.

## Non-goals

* Do not use SOL scores to automatically change routing policy in this child.
* Do not treat provider/QC raw finding counts as worker blame without attribution confidence.
* Do not remove existing diagnosis report fields without a compatibility path.

POL-480.title: Define SOL evidence schema and aggregation inputs
POL-480.body:
## Objective

Define the SOL evidence schema and aggregation model across Foreman, worker, router, QC, validation, token, runtime, and intervention signals.

## Context

Existing artifacts include WorkerResultContract, current-state completed child results, dispatch records, telemetry JSONL, cluster state validation results, run ledgers, Worker Router telemetry planned in [POL-469](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-469/emit-router-telemetry-and-sol-scoring-inputs), and QC artifacts planned in [POL-476](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-476/feed-qc-metrics-into-sol-scoring-and-operator-reports). SOL needs a normalized read model over these sources.

## Goal

Add typed SOL evidence inputs and loaders that can aggregate existing durable artifacts without changing runtime behavior or losing backward compatibility.

## Scope

* src/autoresearch/**
* src/types/**
* src/loop/dispatch-state.ts
* src/loop/checkpoint.ts
* src/cluster-state/types.ts
* src/finalize/run-report.ts
* smartdocs/specs/raw/**
* tests covering SOL evidence loading and schema validation

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] SOL evidence types represent run, child, Foreman, worker, router, QC, validation, token, runtime, and intervention signals.
- [ ] Evidence loading reads existing run artifacts and tolerates absent future router/QC fields by marking them unavailable.
- [ ] Evidence model records grouping keys for repo, route, task type, role, risk, provider, and model when available.
- [ ] No existing WorkerResultContract or result packet consumers break.
- [ ] Tests cover legacy autoresearch runs, runs with completed_children_results, missing telemetry, and future optional QC/router evidence.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/autoresearch src/types src/loop src/cluster-state

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-479](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-479/add-sol-command-surface-and-autoresearch-compatibility-aliases) being Done first.
* Coordinate with [POL-469](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-469/emit-router-telemetry-and-sol-scoring-inputs) and [POL-476](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-476/feed-qc-metrics-into-sol-scoring-and-operator-reports) so this child consumes their eventual output shapes without owning those implementations.

## Non-goals

* Do not implement SOL scoring formulas in this child.
* Do not add router or QC providers in this child.
* Do not mutate run artifacts while loading evidence.

POL-479.title: Add SOL command surface and autoresearch compatibility aliases
POL-479.body:
## Objective

Add the SOL operator-facing command surface while preserving existing autoresearch command compatibility.

## Context

The current CLI exposes `polaris autoresearch score` and `polaris autoresearch propose`. SOL should become the broader name, but existing scripts and docs may still use autoresearch. The migration should be additive first.

## Goal

Expose SOL command aliases and update naming in code/docs so SOL is the parent concept and autoresearch remains a backward-compatible sub-command surface.

## Scope

* src/cli/autoresearch.ts
* src/cli/index.ts
* src/cli/index.test.ts
* src/cli/SUMMARY.md
* src/autoresearch/**
* smartdocs/specs/raw/**

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `polaris sol score <run-id>` works with the same behavior as the current scoring command.
- [ ] `polaris sol propose <diagnosis-file>` works with the same safety gates as the current proposal command.
- [ ] `polaris autoresearch ...` remains available and documented as a compatibility alias.
- [ ] Help text describes SOL as the broader subsystem without breaking existing command expectations.
- [ ] Tests cover both SOL and autoresearch command paths.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/cli src/autoresearch

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-478](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-478/document-sol-architecture-and-auto-research-boundaries) being Done first.

## Non-goals

* Do not remove the autoresearch command surface.
* Do not change scoring semantics in this child.
* Do not add historical storage or recommendation generation in this child.

POL-478.title: Document SOL architecture and Auto Research boundaries
POL-478.body:
## Objective

Document the Self-Optimization Loop architecture and the boundary between SOL and the existing Auto Research sub-capability.

## Context

[POL-462](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-462/analyze-rename-auto-research-loop-to-self-optimization-loop) concluded that SOL should be the broader optimization engine while Auto Research remains a narrower evidence-scoring/proposal capability. This child turns that analysis into repo-local architecture guidance before runtime code changes.

## Goal

Produce SOL architecture documentation covering observation, evaluation, optimization, run timing, data flows, deterministic boundaries, and Polaris-repo versus consumer-repo behavior.

## Scope

* smartdocs/raw/analysis/**
* smartdocs/specs/raw/**
* smartdocs/specs/active/**
* src/autoresearch/POLARIS.md
* src/cli/SUMMARY.md

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] SOL architecture defines observation, evaluation, optimization, and recommendation phases.
- [ ] Auto Research is documented as a SOL sub-capability rather than the parent concept.
- [ ] Boundaries state that SOL recommends and routes review-gated work, not silent runtime mutation.
- [ ] The spec names Worker Router and QC as upstream signal producers, not SOL-owned replacements.
- [ ] Route docs reflect the intended naming and ownership without promoting draft doctrine outside the SmartDocs workflow.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/autoresearch src/cli

## Ordering

* None. This child runs first and informs all later SOL implementation work.

## Non-goals

* Do not implement SOL runtime code in this child.
* Do not remove or rename the `polaris autoresearch` command yet.
* Do not promote docs to active doctrine without the existing SmartDocs promotion workflow.

POL-462.title: ANALYZE: Rename Auto Research Loop to Self-Optimization Loop
